protocol: single development-selected policy with revised wide development-only calibration

calibration_protocol.reason: The original development-only calibration grid was not closed: 26/27 final models selected the maximum class-0 bias, 22/27 selected the maximum class-3 bias, and 24/27 selected the maximum 3+ membership threshold.
calibration_protocol.test_independence: The revised grid and boundary gate are determined from development-set boundary behavior only. No v1 or v2 testA/testB metric is used to choose a revised calibration setting.
calibration_protocol.training_policy: Reuse the already development-selected Stage 5.6 checkpoints. Do not retrain because this revision changes only inference calibration after checkpoint selection.
calibration_protocol.pilot_policy: Recalibrate all five already selected pilot checkpoints on the revised grid and rerun development-only recipe selection. If the winning recipe changes, train and development-select the complete 27-cell grid for that recipe before revised test evaluation.

pretest_boundary_history.detected_before_revised_test: clip_dinov2_5pct_seed0_hierarchical selected class3_bias=8.0, the initial v2 maximum
pretest_boundary_history.first_action: Stopped the calibration run before any revised test evaluation. A development-only probe extended class3_bias to 16.0 and found an interior optimum at 10.0, so the next pretest grid expanded class3 to 16.0.
pretest_boundary_history.near_class0_boundary_check: clip_dinov2_5pct_seed1_hierarchical selected class0_bias=7.5 with the then-common upper bound 8.0. A development-only two-axis probe extended class0_bias to 16.0 while retaining class3_bias through 16.0; the exact same interior optimum (7.5, 7.5, 0.9) and metric values were recovered.
pretest_boundary_history.second_boundary_detection: clip_dinov2_10pct_seed2_hierarchical subsequently selected class0_bias=8.0 at the upper boundary and class3_bias=15.5 one step below the upper boundary. The run was stopped before revised test evaluation.
pretest_boundary_history.final_joint_probe: A development-only joint probe over class0 [-1,16], class3 [0,32], and the locked threshold list selected the interior setting (8.5,15.5,0.9). The final common grid adopts those full ranges for every pilot and final cell.
pretest_boundary_history.threshold_boundary_detection: On the expanded bias grid, clip_dinov2_5pct_seed1_hierarchical selected membership_threshold=0.95 at the then-current upper search boundary. The run was stopped before revised test evaluation.
pretest_boundary_history.full_domain_threshold_probe: A development-only probe covered the complete legal threshold domain [0,1] and added dense high-threshold values. It selected 0.925 with the same primary metrics as 0.95; the declared tie-break preferred 0.925 because it is closer to the neutral 0.5 threshold. The final common grid uses this complete threshold list.

calibration_protocol.calibration_row_storage: Each calibration artifact stores the full grid definition, best setting, evaluation count, and top 100 settings. All 45,500 settings are deterministic from the recorded grid and criterion; omitting the remaining rows avoids hundreds of megabytes of redundant JSON.
calibration_protocol.boundary_gate: Revised test evaluation is blocked if any selected pilot or final-grid calibration chooses a bias boundary or a threshold boundary that does not equal the natural legal endpoint 0 or 1. If the gate fails, expand that axis using development data only, update this lock, and recalibrate before test access. A selected threshold of exactly 0 or 1 is recorded as a natural endpoint rather than a truncatable search boundary.
calibration_protocol.archive_policy: Preserve all original narrow-grid calibrations and test results with the v1_narrow suffix. Revised wide-grid artifacts use the canonical Stage 5.6 paths and identify calibration_revision=v2_wide.
calibration_protocol.reporting_policy: The corrected wide-grid Stage 5.6 table becomes the main result. The original narrow-grid table remains an explicitly archived calibration sensitivity and is never deleted.
